title: Your Agentic Workflow's Cache Keepalive Costs 8x Too Much
excerpt: Everyone pings their prompt cache every 30 seconds. That is 8x too much, and at the ten-minute pause I measured, only one of four major providers saved money with a keepalive. Measured keepalive economics across Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, and DeepSeek.
content_html:
Everyone pings their prompt cache every 30 seconds. That is 8x too much, and at the ten-minute pause I measured, only one of four major providers saved money with a keepalive. Measured keepalive economics across Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, and DeepSeek.

A cache keepalive is the one optimization every agent builder agrees on, and almost everyone runs it at the wrong setting.

The convention is to ping every 30 seconds. That convention costs 8× more than necessary, and the surprise is bigger: at the ten-minute pause I measured, only one of the four major providers saved money with a keepalive. I measured this across Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, and DeepSeek, with a harness that can prove its own timing, and the numbers rearranged a few of my beliefs. The right interval is about 4 minutes, not 30 seconds. A 30 s keepalive loses money at long gaps on every provider I tested. On Anthropic, the 4-minute keepalive saves real money. On DeepSeek it buys latency, not dollars. On OpenAI and Gemini it buys nothing at these gaps. Whether to keep the cache warm is a per-provider decision, not a universal one.

The pause that eats the cache

Provider prompt caches are one of the best deals in the API: send a prefix the server processed a moment ago, and you pay about a tenth of the input price and skip most of the prefill latency. But the cache expires in minutes, and agentic workloads are the worst possible user of it. An agent thinks, acts, waits. It fires a request, runs a build or a test suite or sits on a human approval for ten minutes, and only then sends the follow-up that would have reused the (now large) conversation prefix. The pause outlives the cache. The follow-up pays full price, full latency, and at agent scale this is a real line item.

The defense is well known. Re-send the exact prefix on a timer during the pause; every read refreshes the TTL. Aider shipped it in 2024, Anthropic's docs recommend it, and community posts worked out the Anthropic cost mechanics and even a 4-minute interval on paper. The folklore, it turns out, had the right answer. What it didn't have was measurement. Everything above is practice and arithmetic. Everything below is measured: four providers, two prefix sizes, idle gaps to ten minutes, three independent runs, every call timestamped.

Credit where the project started: in a conference room in Bellevue, at AgentSys, where I saw Haiying Shen and Simon Peter present CacheWise, their study of KV-cache management for coding agents. Their trace analysis shows from the serving side exactly the problem above: agent sessions reuse enormous prefixes, and naive eviction wrecks them. Their question was how the server should manage the cache for agents; mine became what the client can do about it on its own. The keepalive is the client's answer, and this study is my thanks for the inspiration.

Providers come in three regimes

  • Hard TTL (Anthropic). The baseline is warm through 5 minutes and dead at 10: 0 of 48 samples across three runs. The keepalive holds 40 of 40. Eviction here is a cliff, exactly as documented, and the keepalive is a money decision.
  • Lossy (DeepSeek via DeepInfra). The baseline leaks at every gap and is gone at 10 minutes (4 of 48). The keepalive holds 42 of 42 there but itself misses about 20% at 1 minute, because a router endpoint pin is not a machine pin: the ping warms one machine, the follow-up lands on another.
  • Sticky (OpenAI, Google). Baselines survive 10 minutes most of the time (OpenAI 39/48, Google 20/24). There is little to defend. The keepalive is variance removal, and at short gaps it is pure waste.

The interval is the whole game

Here is the entire economics of the keepalive, and it is settled arithmetic:

  1. A ping costs the read price (≈10% of input) every interval τ. Holding a prefix costs 0.1× per τ.
  2. A re-prefill costs 100% once (125% on Anthropic, which bills the cache write).
  3. So spend per hour falls as 1/τ, and the interval buys nothing except TTL safety. The optimal interval is the largest one safely under the TTL: τ* = TTL − margin ≈ 4 minutes at Anthropic's 5-minute TTL.
  4. Break-even is idle ≈ τ(w/r − 1): about 46 minutes at Anthropic's prices, 36 for OpenAI and DeepSeek, 12 for Google (its cached reads are 0.25×, not 0.1×). Past that, stop pinging: let the cache die and pay the re-prefill.

That last one is the rule worth internalizing, so here it is as insurance. Every ping is a premium you pay to avoid one claim: the re-prefill. On Anthropic the premium is 0.1× the input price and the claim is 1.25×, so the claim is worth 12.5 premiums. Pay one premium every 4 minutes and you can afford twelve of them before the premiums exceed the claim. Twelve premiums at 4 minutes apart is 46 minutes of pause. That is the line.

Concretely, on a 100k-token Anthropic prefix (about $0.30 of input at list price): each ping costs $0.03, and a cold re-prefill costs $0.38.

  • 10-minute pause: two pings, $0.06, and you skip the $0.38. Keep it warm. You are 6× ahead.
  • 46-minute pause: eleven pings, $0.33, versus the $0.38 re-prefill. Roughly a wash. This is the line.
  • 1-hour pause: fifteen pings, $0.45, to avoid a $0.38 re-prefill. Stop pinging. Let the cache go cold and pay the re-prefill when you come back.

The formula tells you where your own line sits once you plug in your prices and interval. The point is that there is a line, it is closer than people assume, and past it the disciplined move is to stop paying premiums on a policy you will never claim.

The 30-second convention everyone copies has a break-even of about 6 minutes, which is why it loses money at a 10-minute gap on all four providers. I ran the 4-minute arm too: pings fired at +240.1s and +480.1s (0.1s drift), held 23 of 24 samples warm, and cost 7.8× less than the 30-second arm for the same warmth. The convention isn't cautious. It's just expensive.

600s idle, 100k prefixLet it dieKeepalive 30sKeepalive 240s
Anthropic Sonnet 4.5$0.667 (cold)$0.867 (loses money)$0.414 (saves 38%)
OpenAI GPT-5.1$0.115 (usually warm anyway)$0.317 (loses)$0.136 (loses)
DeepSeek V3.2$0.043 (cold, 5.4s TTFT)$0.249 (loses)$0.060 (loses; but ~3× faster)
Gemini 2.5 Pro$0.131 (usually warm anyway)$0.649 (loses)$0.186 (loses)

Read the last column again: at this gap, Anthropic is the only provider where the keepalive saves money. That is not a coincidence of pricing. Anthropic is the only provider with all three ingredients: a hard TTL (eviction at 10 minutes is certain, so the insurance always pays out), a re-prefill that costs extra (its 1.25× cache-write premium raises what you avoid), and cheap 0.1× reads (the premiums are low). Each of the others breaks one leg: OpenAI and Gemini rarely evict at these gaps (nothing to insure), DeepSeek's re-prefill costs four cents (nothing worth insuring), and Gemini's 0.25× reads make the premiums expensive. The qualifier matters, though: this is one measured point, not the whole curve. At a 15 or 20 minute pause, past OpenAI's sticky window but inside its 36-minute break-even, its keepalive plausibly pays too; the break-even formula predicts it, and I have not measured it. What the formula and the data agree on is where each provider's paying band starts and ends, and that Anthropic's band is the widest and the most certain. It also happens to be the provider running most agentic workloads, and in a long session the savings compound: every tool call past the TTL is another avoided re-prefill.

When not to keepalive at all

This part surprised me, and it is the part the folklore misses. OpenAI: the cache is sticky (39 of 48 samples warm at 10 minutes with no help), so the keepalive insures variance, not eviction; at these gaps it isn't worth the pings. DeepSeek: a cold re-prefill is so cheap ($0.043 for 100k) that even the 4-minute keepalive only breaks even on cost. What it buys is latency (5.4s → 1.4–2.0s to first token), so it's a latency play, not a money play. Gemini: the worst case for the strategy, and I measured it to be sure: even the 4-minute keepalive costs 40% more than just letting the cache die ($0.186 vs $0.131 at 100k), because Gemini combines a sticky baseline (little to insure) with 0.25× cached reads (expensive insurance), no affinity lever (hits are a machine lottery with a minute of commit delay), and a 12-minute break-even horizon. The prescription is per-provider, not universal.

What is settled, and what I believe

Settled: the arithmetic above, the measured retention curves, the measured 7.8×, and the break-even horizons. Those you can check against the data, which is public with the harness.

Belief: what happens when everyone takes this advice. A cache tier's eviction policy ranks by expected reuse; a keepalive manufactures recency, so once every client pings, LRU has nothing left to rank and the tier degrades for everyone. Residency today is priced per read, not per token-hour held, so each operator's rational rent payment imposes an unpriced cost on every other tenant. My prediction: keepalive adoption forces providers to meter residency directly. Google's explicit cache already bills per token-hour, and Anthropic's 1-hour tier at 2× write is a step in the same direction. The arbitrage is real, and it has an expiry date.

The policy

  • Keep warm only across bounded, plausibly reused pauses (a tool call, an approval wait), at τ* = TTL − margin. About 4 minutes at Anthropic, about 50 on the 1-hour tier.
  • Abandon the keepalive past the break-even horizon (≈46 minutes at Anthropic prices). Let it die, re-prefill, walk away.
  • Know your provider's regime. At today's prices and gaps: Anthropic saves money, DeepSeek buys latency only, OpenAI and Gemini buy nothing. One honest caveat: OpenAI's TTL is "5 to 10 minutes," and our 10-minute gap sat inside its sticky window; somewhere past that there may be a band where its keepalive pays. We did not measure it, so we do not claim it.
  • Skip it below the TTL, and on providers whose cache is sticky (OpenAI, Gemini) or whose re-prefill is trivially cheap (DeepSeek, unless you are buying latency).
  • Never keep a dead session warm. The pi agent harness implements exactly this: pings only while a tool batch runs, default off.

Keep it warm, briefly, at the right rate. Ping less.

References

  • Shubham Tiwari, Tapan Chugh, Nash Rickert, Simon Peter, Ratul Mahajan, Haiying Shen. CacheWise: Understanding Workloads and Optimizing KVCache Management for Efficiently Serving LLM Coding Agents (the talk that inspired this project).
  • Maxim Khailo. Keeping the Cache Warm Pays: Keepalive Economics for Agentic Workloads (paper, PDF) and the harness + data (scripts/cache-research).
  • Aider AI. Change history (cache keepalive, v0.53.0, 2024).
  • Anthropic. Prompt caching (pre-warming guidance).
  • Veritas Supera. We Taught Our AI Agents to Take Coffee Breaks (the 4-minute interval, on paper).
  • Brandon Wie. Anthropic Prompt Cache TTL + Cost Mechanics.
  • OpenClaw. Cache keep-warm pings proposal (TTL-proportional intervals).
  • Don't Break the Cache (what to cache for agentic tasks, a different question).
  • Prompt cache thrashing (the commons externality).

blocks.html_en: The defense is well known. Re-send the exact prefix on a timer during the pause; every read refreshes the TTL. Aider shipped it in 2024, Anthropic's docs recommend it, and community posts worked out the Anthropic cost mechanics and even a 4-minute interval on paper. The folklore, it turns out, had the right answer. What it didn't have was measurement. Everything above is practice and arithmetic. Everything below is measured: four providers, two prefix sizes, idle gaps to ten minutes, three independent runs, every call timestamped.

sentences.en: The defense is well known.

sentences.en: Re-send the exact prefix on a timer during the pause; every read refreshes the TTL.
sentences.zh: 在暂停期间重新发送计时器上的确切前缀；每次读取都会刷新 TTL。

sentences.en: Aider shipped it in 2024, Anthropic's docs recommend it, and community posts worked out the Anthropic cost mechanics and even a 4-minute interval on paper.
sentences.zh: Aider 在 2024 年发布了它，Anthropic 的文档推荐了它，社区帖子制定了 Anthropic 成本机制，甚至在纸上确定了 4 分钟的间隔。

sentences.en: The folklore, it turns out, had the right answer.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，民间传说有正确的答案。

sentences.en: What it didn't have was measurement.
sentences.zh: 它没有测量。

sentences.en: Everything above is practice and arithmetic.
sentences.zh: 以上一切都是练习和算术。

sentences.en: Everything below is measured: four providers, two prefix sizes, idle gaps to ten minutes, three independent runs, every call timestamped.
sentences.zh: 以下所有内容均经过测量：四个提供商、两个前缀大小、十分钟的空闲间隙、三个独立运行、每个调用都带有时间戳。

blocks.html_en: Credit where the project started: in a conference room in Bellevue, at AgentSys, where I saw Haiying Shen and Simon Peter present CacheWise, their study of KV-cache management for coding agents. Their trace analysis shows from the serving side exactly the problem above: agent sessions reuse enormous prefixes, and naive eviction wrecks them. Their question was how the server should manage the cache for agents; mine became what the client can do about it on its own. The keepalive is the client's answer, and this study is my thanks for the inspiration.

sentences.en: Credit where the project started: in a conference room in Bellevue, at AgentSys, where I saw Haiying Shen and Simon Peter present CacheWise, their study of KV-cache management for coding agents.

sentences.en: Their trace analysis shows from the serving side exactly the problem above: agent sessions reuse enormous prefixes, and naive eviction wrecks them.
sentences.zh: 他们的跟踪分析从服务端准确地显示了上述问题：代理会话重复使用大量前缀，而天真的驱逐会破坏它们。

blocks.html_en: Providers come in three regimes

sentences.en: Providers come in three regimes

sentences.en: Warm rate (fraction of samples whose post-idle request hit cache) vs idle gap, 100k prefix.
sentences.zh: 热率（空闲后请求命中缓存的样本比例）与空闲间隙，100k 前缀。

sentences.en: Dashed: idle baseline.

blocks.html_en: • Hard TTL (Anthropic). The baseline is warm through 5 minutes and dead at 10: 0 of 48 samples across three runs. The keepalive holds 40 of 40. Eviction here is a cliff, exactly as documented, and the keepalive is a money decision.

sentences.en: Hard TTL (Anthropic).
sentences.zh: 硬 TTL（人择）。

sentences.en: The baseline is warm through 5 minutes and dead at 10: 0 of 48 samples across three runs.
sentences.zh: 3 次运行中 48 个样本的基线在 5 分钟内保持温暖，并在 10:0 处死亡。

sentences.en: The keepalive holds 40 of 40.
sentences.zh: keepalive 保留了 40 个中的 40 个。

sentences.en: Eviction here is a cliff, exactly as documented, and the keepalive is a money decision.
sentences.zh: 这里的驱逐是一个悬崖，正如记录的那样，而保活是一个金钱决定。

blocks.html_en: • Lossy (DeepSeek via DeepInfra). The baseline leaks at every gap and is gone at 10 minutes (4 of 48). The keepalive holds 42 of 42 there but itself misses about 20% at 1 minute, because a router endpoint pin is not a machine pin: the ping warms one machine, the follow-up lands on another.

sentences.en: Lossy (DeepSeek via DeepInfra).
sentences.zh: 有损（DeepSeek，通过 DeepInfra）。

sentences.en: The baseline leaks at every gap and is gone at 10 minutes (4 of 48).
sentences.zh: 基线在每个间隙处都会泄漏，并在 10 分钟后消失（48 分钟中的 4 分钟）。

sentences.en: The keepalive holds 42 of 42 there but itself misses about 20% at 1 minute, because a router endpoint pin is not a machine pin: the ping warms one machine, the follow-up lands on another.

blocks.html_en: • Sticky (OpenAI, Google). Baselines survive 10 minutes most of the time (OpenAI 39/48, Google 20/24). There is little to defend. The keepalive is variance removal, and at short gaps it is pure waste.

sentences.en: Sticky (OpenAI, Google).
sentences.zh: 粘性（OpenAI、谷歌）。

sentences.en: Baselines survive 10 minutes most of the time (OpenAI 39/48, Google 20/24).

blocks.html_en: The interval is the whole game

sentences.en: The interval is the whole game
sentences.zh: 间歇就是整场比赛

blocks.html_en: Here is the entire economics of the keepalive, and it is settled arithmetic:

sentences.en: Here is the entire economics of the keepalive, and it is settled arithmetic:
sentences.zh: 这是保活的整个经济学，并且是经过算术计算的：

blocks.html_en: • A ping costs the read price (≈10% of input) every interval τ. Holding a prefix costs 0.1× per τ.

sentences.en: A ping costs the read price (≈10% of input) every interval τ.
sentences.zh: 每间隔 τ 一次 ping 都会花费读取价格（约输入的 10%）。

sentences.en: Holding a prefix costs 0.1× per τ.
sentences.zh: 持有一个前缀的成本为每 τ 0.1×。

blocks.html_en: • A re-prefill costs 100% once (125% on Anthropic, which bills the cache write).

sentences.en: A re-prefill costs 100% once (125% on Anthropic, which bills the cache write).
sentences.zh: 重新预填充一次费用为 100%（Anthropic 为 125%，对缓存写入进行计费）。

blocks.html_en: • So spend per hour falls as 1/τ, and the interval buys nothing except TTL safety. The optimal interval is the largest one safely under the TTL: τ* = TTL − margin ≈ 4 minutes at Anthropic's 5-minute TTL.

sentences.en: So spend per hour falls as 1/τ, and the interval buys nothing except TTL safety.
sentences.zh: 因此，每小时的花费下降为 1/τ，并且除了 TTL 安全性之外，间隔时间什么也买不到。

sentences.en: The optimal interval is the largest one safely under the TTL: τ* = TTL − margin ≈ 4 minutes at Anthropic's 5-minute TTL.
sentences.zh: 最佳间隔是 TTL 下安全的最大间隔：τ* = TTL − 余量 ≈ 4 分钟（在 Anthropic 的 5 分钟 TTL 下）。

blocks.html_en: • Break-even is idle ≈ τ(w/r − 1): about 46 minutes at Anthropic's prices, 36 for OpenAI and DeepSeek, 12 for Google (its cached reads are 0.25×, not 0.1×). Past that, stop pinging: let the cache die and pay the re-prefill.

sentences.en: Break-even is idle ≈ τ(w/r − 1): about 46 minutes at Anthropic's prices, 36 for OpenAI and DeepSeek, 12 for Google (its cached reads are 0.25×, not 0.1×).
sentences.zh: 盈亏平衡空闲 ≈ τ(w/r − 1)：以 Anthropic 的价格计算大约 46 分钟，OpenAI 和 DeepSeek 为 36 分钟，Google 为 12 分钟（其缓存读取是 0.25×，而不是 0.1×）。

sentences.en: Past that, stop pinging: let the cache die and pay the re-prefill.
sentences.zh: 之后，停止 ping：让缓存失效并支付重新预填充的费用。

blocks.html_en: That last one is the rule worth internalizing, so here it is as insurance. Every ping is a premium you pay to avoid one claim: the re-prefill. On Anthropic the premium is 0.1× the input price and the claim is 1.25×, so the claim is worth 12.5 premiums. Pay one premium every 4 minutes and you can afford twelve of them before the premiums exceed the claim. Twelve premiums at 4 minutes apart is 46 minutes of pause. That is the line.

sentences.en: That last one is the rule worth internalizing, so here it is as insurance.
sentences.zh: 最后一条是值得内化的规则，所以在这里它是作为保险。

sentences.en: Every ping is a premium you pay to avoid one claim: the re-prefill.
sentences.zh: 每一次 ping 都是您为避免一项索赔而支付的费用：重新预充。

sentences.en: On Anthropic the premium is 0.1× the input price and the claim is 1.25×, so the claim is worth 12.5 premiums.
sentences.zh: 在 Anthropic 上，保费是投入价格的 0.1 倍，索赔是 1.25 倍，因此索赔价值 12.5 倍保费。

sentences.en: Pay one premium every 4 minutes and you can afford twelve of them before the premiums exceed the claim.
sentences.zh: 每 4 分钟支付一笔保费，在保费超过索赔额之前，您可以负担十二次保费。

sentences.en: Twelve premiums at 4 minutes apart is 46 minutes of pause.
sentences.zh: 间隔 4 分钟的 12 场比赛是 46 分钟的暂停。

sentences.en: That is the line.
sentences.zh: 这就是那条线。

blocks.html_en: Concretely, on a 100k-token Anthropic prefix (about $0.30 of input at list price): each ping costs $0.03, and a cold re-prefill costs $0.38.

sentences.en: Concretely, on a 100k-token Anthropic prefix (about $0.30 of input at list price): each ping costs $0.03, and a cold re-prefill costs $0.38.

blocks.html_en: • 10-minute pause: two pings, $0.06, and you skip the $0.38. Keep it warm. You are 6× ahead.

blocks.html_en: • 46-minute pause: eleven pings, $0.33, versus the $0.38 re-prefill. Roughly a wash. This is the line.

sentences.zh: 46 分钟暂停：11 次 ping，费用为 0.33 美元，而重新预填充费用为 0.38 美元。

blocks.html_en: • 1-hour pause: fifteen pings, $0.45, to avoid a $0.38 re-prefill. Stop pinging. Let the cache go cold and pay the re-prefill when you come back.

sentences.en: 1-hour pause: fifteen pings, $0.45, to avoid a $0.38 re-prefill.

blocks.html_en: The formula tells you where your own line sits once you plug in your prices and interval. The point is that there is a line, it is closer than people assume, and past it the disciplined move is to stop paying premiums on a policy you will never claim.

sentences.en: The formula tells you where your own line sits once you plug in your prices and interval.

blocks.html_en: The 30-second convention everyone copies has a break-even of about 6 minutes, which is why it loses money at a 10-minute gap on all four providers. I ran the 4-minute arm too: pings fired at +240.1s and +480.1s (0.1s drift), held 23 of 24 samples warm, and cost 7.8× less than the 30-second arm for the same warmth. The convention isn't cautious. It's just expensive.

Read the last column again: at this gap, Anthropic is the only provider where the keepalive saves money. That is not a coincidence of pricing. Anthropic is the only provider with all three ingredients: a hard TTL (eviction at 10 minutes is certain, so the insurance always pays out), a re-prefill that costs extra (its 1.25× cache-write premium raises what you avoid), and cheap 0.1× reads (the premiums are low). Each of the others breaks one leg: OpenAI and Gemini rarely evict at these gaps (nothing to insure), DeepSeek's re-prefill costs four cents (nothing worth insuring), and Gemini's 0.25× reads make the premiums expensive. The qualifier matters, though: this is one measured point, not the whole curve. At a 15 or 20 minute pause, past OpenAI's sticky window but inside its 36-minute break-even, its keepalive plausibly pays too; the break-even formula predicts it, and I have not measured it. What the formula and the data agree on is where each provider's paying band starts and ends, and that Anthropic's band is the widest and the most certain. It also happens to be the provider running most agentic workloads, and in a long session the savings compound: every tool call past the TTL is another avoided re-prefill.

blocks.html_en: This part surprised me, and it is the part the folklore misses. OpenAI: the cache is sticky (39 of 48 samples warm at 10 minutes with no help), so the keepalive insures variance, not eviction; at these gaps it isn't worth the pings. DeepSeek: a cold re-prefill is so cheap ($0.043 for 100k) that even the 4-minute keepalive only breaks even on cost. What it buys is latency (5.4s → 1.4–2.0s to first token), so it's a latency play, not a money play. Gemini: the worst case for the strategy, and I measured it to be sure: even the 4-minute keepalive costs 40% more than just letting the cache die ($0.186 vs $0.131 at 100k), because Gemini combines a sticky baseline (little to insure) with 0.25× cached reads (expensive insurance), no affinity lever (hits are a machine lottery with a minute of commit delay), and a 12-minute break-even horizon. The prescription is per-provider, not universal.

blocks.html_en: Belief: what happens when everyone takes this advice. A cache tier's eviction policy ranks by expected reuse; a keepalive manufactures recency, so once every client pings, LRU has nothing left to rank and the tier degrades for everyone. Residency today is priced per read, not per token-hour held, so each operator's rational rent payment imposes an unpriced cost on every other tenant. My prediction: keepalive adoption forces providers to meter residency directly. Google's explicit cache already bills per token-hour, and Anthropic's 1-hour tier at 2× write is a step in the same direction. The arbitrage is real, and it has an expiry date.

blocks.html_en: • Keep warm only across bounded, plausibly reused pauses (a tool call, an approval wait), at τ* = TTL − margin. About 4 minutes at Anthropic, about 50 on the 1-hour tier.

We did not measure it, so we do not claim it.

content_html_zh:
每个人每 30 秒 ping 一次提示缓存。 这多了 8 倍，而且在我测量的 10 分钟暂停中，四家主要提供商中只有一家通过 keepalive 节省了资金。 测量了 Anthropic、OpenAI、Gemini 和 DeepSeek 的保活经济性。

缓存保活是每个代理构建者都同意的一项优化，但几乎每个人都在错误的设置下运行它。

约定是每 30 秒 ping 一次。 该约定的成本比必要的高出 8 倍，而且令人惊讶的是：在我测量的 10 分钟暂停中，四大主要提供商中只有一家通过保持活动节省了资金。 我在 Anthropic、OpenAI、Gemini 和 DeepSeek 上进行了测量，并使用了可以证明其自身时机的工具，这些数字重新排列了我的一些信念。 正确的间隔时间约为 4 分钟，而不是 30 秒。 在我测试的每个提供商上，30 秒的保活时间在较长的间隙中都会造成损失。 在 Anthropic 上，4 分钟的保活确实可以节省金钱。 在 DeepSeek 上，它购买的是延迟，而不是金钱。 在 OpenAI 和 Gemini 上，它不会在这些差距上购买任何东西。 是否保持缓存温暖是每个提供商的决定，而不是普遍的决定。

消耗缓存的暂停

提供者提示缓存是 API 中最好的交易之一：发送服务器刚才处理的前缀，您只需支付大约十分之一的输入价格，并跳过大部分预填充延迟。 但缓存在几分钟内就会过期，而代理工作负载可能是最糟糕的用户。 代理人思考、行动、等待。 它会发出请求，运行构建或测试套件，或者等待人工批准十分钟，然后才发送将重用（现在很大）对话前缀的后续内容。 暂停的寿命比缓存的寿命长。 后续支付全价、全延迟，并且在代理规模上，这是一个真正的订单项。

防守是众所周知的。 在暂停期间重新发送计时器上的确切前缀；每次读取都会刷新 TTL。 Aider 在 2024 年发布了它，Anthropic 的文档推荐了它，社区帖子制定了 Anthropic 成本机制，甚至在纸上确定了 4 分钟的间隔。 事实证明，民间传说有正确的答案。 它没有测量。 以上一切都是练习和算术。 以下所有内容均经过测量：四个提供商、两个前缀大小、十分钟的空闲间隙、三个独立运行、每个调用都带有时间戳。

项目开始的地方：在贝尔维尤的 AgentSys 会议室，我看到 Haiying Shen 和 Simon Peter 展示了 CacheWise，这是他们对编码代理的 KV 缓存管理的研究。 他们的跟踪分析从服务端准确地显示了上述问题：代理会话重复使用大量前缀，而天真的驱逐会破坏它们。 他们的问题是服务器应该如何管理代理的缓存；我的变成了客户可以自己做的事情。 keepalive是客户的答案，这个研究是我感谢的启发。

提供者分为三种制度

  • 硬 TTL（人择）。 3 次运行中 48 个样本的基线在 5 分钟内保持温暖，并在 10:0 处死亡。 keepalive 保留了 40 个中的 40 个。 这里的驱逐是一个悬崖，正如记录的那样，而保活是一个金钱决定。
  • 有损（DeepSeek，通过 DeepInfra）。 基线在每个间隙处都会泄漏，并在 10 分钟后消失（48 分钟中的 4 分钟）。 keepalive 保留了 42 个中的 42 个，但在 1 分钟内丢失了大约 20%，因为路由器端点 pin 不是机器 pin：ping 会预热一台机器，后续的操作会落在另一台机器上。
  • 粘性（OpenAI、谷歌）。 大多数情况下，基线可以存活 10 分钟（OpenAI 39/48，Google 20/24）。 没什么可辩护的。 keepalive 是方差消除，在短间隙中它纯粹是浪费。

间歇就是整场比赛

这是保活的整个经济学，并且是经过算术计算的：

  1. 每间隔 τ 一次 ping 都会花费读取价格（约输入的 10%）。 持有一个前缀的成本为每 τ 0.1×。
  2. 重新预填充一次费用为 100%（Anthropic 为 125%，对缓存写入进行计费）。
  3. 因此，每小时的花费下降为 1/τ，并且除了 TTL 安全性之外，间隔时间什么也买不到。 最佳间隔是 TTL 下安全的最大间隔：τ* = TTL − 余量 ≈ 4 分钟（在 Anthropic 的 5 分钟 TTL 下）。
  4. 盈亏平衡空闲 ≈ τ(w/r − 1)：以 Anthropic 的价格计算大约 46 分钟，OpenAI 和 DeepSeek 为 36 分钟，Google 为 12 分钟（其缓存读取是 0.25×，而不是 0.1×）。 之后，停止 ping：让缓存失效并支付重新预填充的费用。

最后一条是值得内化的规则，所以在这里它是作为保险。 每一次 ping 都是您为避免一项索赔而支付的费用：重新预充。 在 Anthropic 上，保费是投入价格的 0.1 倍，索赔是 1.25 倍，因此索赔价值 12.5 倍保费。 每 4 分钟支付一笔保费，在保费超过索赔额之前，您可以负担十二次保费。 间隔 4 分钟的 12 场比赛是 46 分钟的暂停。 这就是那条线。

具体来说，对于 10 万代币的 Anthropic 前缀（按标价输入约 0.30 美元）：每个 ping 成本为 0.03 美元，冷重新预填充成本为 0.38 美元。

  • 10 分钟暂停：两次 ping，0.06 美元，然后您跳过 0.38 美元。 保持温暖。 您领先 6 倍。
  • 46 分钟暂停：11 次 ping，费用为 0.33 美元，而重新预填充费用为 0.38 美元。 大致洗了一下。 就是这条线。
  • 1 小时暂停：15 次 ping，0.45 美元，以避免 0.38 美元的重新预充。 停止 ping。 让缓存变冷并在您回来时支付重新预填充的费用。

一旦您输入价格和间隔，该公式就会告诉您自己的线位于何处。 关键是有一条线，它比人们想象的更近，超过它的纪律行动是停止为您永远不会索赔的保单支付保费。

每个人都复制的 30 秒惯例的盈亏平衡点约为 6 分钟，这就是为什么它在所有四个提供商的 10 分钟差距时都亏损的原因。 我也运行了 4 分钟臂：ping 发射时间为 +240.1 秒和 +480.1 秒（0.1 秒漂移），对 24 个样本中的 23 个进行了保温，并且在相同的温度下，成本比 30 秒臂少 7.8 倍。 大会并不谨慎。 只是很贵。

600s 空闲，100k 前缀让它死吧保活30秒保持连接 240 秒
人择十四行诗 4.5$0.667 (cold)$0.867（赔钱）$0.414 (saves 38%)
OpenAI GPT-5.10.115 美元（无论如何通常都是热的）$0.317 (loses)$0.136 (loses)
深寻V3.20.043 美元（冷，5.4s TTFT）$0.249 (loses)$0.060（损失；但快约 3 倍）
双子座2.5专业版0.131 美元（无论如何通常都是热的）$0.649 (loses)$0.186 (loses)

再次阅读上一篇专栏文章：在这个差距上，Anthropic 是唯一可以通过 keepalive 节省资金的提供商。 这并不是定价的巧合。 Anthropic 是唯一一家具备所有这三个要素的提供商：硬 TTL（10 分钟内驱逐是肯定的，因此保险总是赔付）、需要额外费用的重新预填充（其 1.25× 缓存写入溢价提高了您避免的费用）以及便宜的 0.1× 读取（溢价很低）。 其他人都折断了一条腿：OpenAI 和 Gemini 很少在这些间隙处驱逐（没有什么可保险的），DeepSeek 的重新预填充成本为 4 美分（没有什么值得保险的），而 Gemini 的 0.25× 读取使得保费昂贵。 不过，限定符很重要：这是一个测量点，而不是整条曲线。 在 15 或 20 分钟的停顿中，过了 OpenAI 的粘性窗口，但在 36 分钟的收支平衡内，它的 keepalive 似乎也能带来回报；盈亏平衡公式预测了它，但我没有测量它。 公式和数据一致的是每个提供商的付费范围的起始和结束位置，并且 Anthropic 的范围是最宽和最确定的。 它也恰好是运行大多数代理工作负载的提供者，并且在长时间的会话中，节省的复合物：超过 TTL 的每个工具调用都是另一个避免的重新预填充。

什么时候根本不应该保持活动状态

这部分让我感到惊讶，也是民间传说所遗漏的部分。 OpenAI：缓存是粘性的（48 个样本中的 39 个样本在 10 分钟内处于热状态，没有任何帮助），因此 keepalive 确保了方差，而不是驱逐；在这些间隙中，不值得进行 ping 操作。 DeepSeek：冷重新预填充非常便宜（10 万美元 0.043 美元），即使 4 分钟的保持活动也只能实现成本平衡。 它买的是延迟（5.4s → 1.4–2.0s 到第一个代币），所以这是一个延迟游戏，而不是金钱游戏。 Gemini：该策略的最坏情况，我测量了它以确保：即使是 4 分钟的 keepalive 成本也比仅仅让缓存死亡要高 40%（100k 时为 0.186 美元 vs 0.131 美元），因为 Gemini 将粘性基线（几乎无法确保）与 0.25× 缓存读取（昂贵的保险）、没有亲和力杠杆（命中是带有一分钟提交延迟的机器彩票）和12 分钟盈亏平衡范围。 处方是针对每个提供者的，而不是通用的。

已定的事和我所相信的事

解决：上述算术、测量的保留曲线、测量的 7.8× 以及盈亏平衡范围。 您可以根据数据进行检查，这些数据是通过线束公开的。

信念：当每个人都接受这个建议时会发生什么。 缓存层的逐出策略按预期重用进行排名； keepalive 会产生新近度，因此一旦每个客户端执行 ping 操作，LRU 就没有剩余的内容可以排名，并且每个人的级别都会降级。 如今的居住权是按每次阅读定价，而不是按持有的象征性小时定价，因此每个运营商合理的租金支付都会给其他租户带来未定价的成本。 我的预测：保活的采用迫使提供商直接计量居住情况。 Google 的显式缓存已经按令牌小时计费，而 Anthropic 的 2 次写入的 1 小时层级也是朝同一方向迈出的一步。 套利是真实的，并且有到期日。

政策

  • 仅在 τ* = TTL − 裕度时，在有界的、合理重用的暂停（工具调用、批准等待）期间保持温暖。 Anthropic 约 4 分钟，1 小时约 50 分钟。
  • 超过收支平衡范围后放弃保活（按照人为价格计算约为 46 分钟）。 让它消亡，重新填充，走开。
  • 了解您的提供者的制度。 按照今天的价格和差距：Anthropic 省钱，DeepSeek 只购买延迟，OpenAI 和 Gemini 什么也不买。 一个诚实的警告：OpenAI 的 TTL 是“5 到 10 分钟”，而我们的 10 分钟间隙位于其粘性窗口内；在过去的某个地方，可能会有一个乐队，其保活是值得的。 我们没有测量它，所以我们不声称它。
  • 在 TTL 以下以及缓存具有粘性的提供商（OpenAI、Gemini）或其重新预填充非常便宜的提供商（DeepSeek，除非您购买延迟）上跳过它。
  • 永远不要让死会话保持温暖。 pi 代理工具正是实现了这一点：仅在工具批处理运行时执行 ping 操作，默认关闭。

以适当的速度短暂地保持温暖。 平少。
